page: Microsoft Azure D32ads_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the DADSv5 family. It is equipped with an AMD EPYC 7763 processor featuring 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs with dedicated allocation. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and supports hyper-threading. Memory capacity stands at 128.0 GB, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The server includes 1229 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, though it does not feature any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2712.77 and a PassMark memory score of 2898.2, both placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. Multi-core performance is average, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 43242.33 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 14274.0. Large-block memory bandwidth is measured at 77.67 GB/sec for read-only operations and 44.95 GB/sec for write operations. For specialized tasks, the server demonstrates strong performance in image processing and text generation using small 135M LLM models, though prompt processing for large 70B LLM models falls into the bottom 25% at 6.55 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D32ads_v5 offers a balanced resource density with its 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and substantial local storage capacity, making it a viable option for workloads requiring local scratch space without the premium cost of memory-optimized hardware. However, the lack of GPU acceleration and weak performance on large-scale language models present clear tradeoffs for deep learning tasks. This server is suited for general-purpose workloads, database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale text or image processing.
description: The Microsoft Azure D32ads_v5 is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores running at 3.525 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core, alongside 1229 GB of local storage with premium storage capabilities. Benchmarks reveal strong single-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside average multi-core and database execution capabilities. While it excels at image processing and text generation with smaller language models, it exhibits weak performance on large-scale LLM inference and lacks GPU acceleration. This server is suited for database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring a balance of compute, memory, and local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ads_v5 is a general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core and average multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ads_v5 features 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage for balanced general-purpose and database workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 server combining dedicated AMD EPYC processing, 128 GB of RAM, and substantial local storage for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7763 with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores
Equipped with 128.0 GB RAM and 1229 GB of premium-capable local storage
Strong single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
Average multi-core execution, database operations, and web serving throughput
Suited for database hosting, software compilation, and general-purpose workloads
